data.title: Cytosorbents Is Maintained at Buy by D. Boral Capital
Corn Futures Sink on Higher-Than-Expected Supply Outlook — Daily Grain Highlights
data.description:
By Kirk Maltais

  • Corn for December delivery fell 2.6% to $4.30 1/4 a bushel on the Chicago Board of Trade Friday, with traders reversing course on a recent rally in corn after the USDA only cut U.S. corn yields by 0.7 bushels an acre, counter to the argument that crop disease deeply impacted those yields.
  • Soybeans for January delivery fell 2.1% to $11.22 1/2 a bushel.
  • Wheat for December delivery fell 1.7% to $5.26 3/4 a bushel.

HIGHLIGHTS

Stubborn Supply: Grain futures fell after the USDA published its latest WASDE report, with supply figures forecast by the agency reduced less than anticipated by analysts. Grain supplies look plentiful in general, especially for U.S. corn.

"Surprisingly high corn production which drove U.S. carryout above expectations," said Doug Bergman of RCM Alternatives in a note. He added that the USDA's world projections also pegged global corn stocks-to-use levels as "historically low."

Not Encouraged: A separate list of flash sales released by the USDA on Friday, covering sales during the government shutdown, also pressured grain futures.

China was reported as buying soybeans during the shutdown, but only 332,000 metric tons. That is a fraction of the 12 million tons that China promised to buy before the end of the year.

There were numerous sales to "unknown destinations" listed, and those sales could be to China, but they also might not be. "

U.S. production numbers [are] still impressive, balance tables well-stocked, and trade doesn't see any massive flash sales to China to boot," said Matt Zeller of StoneX in a note.

Heightened Fight: Prior to the WASDE's release, CBOT wheat climbed in reaction to a Ukrainian drone strike that hit the Russian port of Novorossiysk, a big hub for Russia's wheat exports. The strike gave wheat fuel to catch up with the gains in corn and soybeans Friday morning, ahead of the WASDE.

Those gains disappeared after the report was released, with higher-than-expected supply figures putting an end to any rallying.

INSIGHT

Future Fixes: The smaller-than-expected adjustment to 2025-26 U.S. corn production and yields won't be the last word the USDA gives on the size of the record crop.

"I think the trade will believe a bigger yield cut could take place in January," said Brian Hoops of Midwest Market Solutions. The USDA may not have accounted for the strength of U.S. corn's export demand, he said, or the consumption of corn by the ethanol industry.

Last week, the EIA reported record-high average daily ethanol production in the U.S., at a pace of 1.123 million barrels a day.

In Other News: The U.S. dollar recovered from early lows Friday, even as equity indexes keep falling.

Wall Street continues to grapple with uncertainty, especially when it comes to missing official data.

The next Fed move is getting less clear, with increasing bets on a pause in interest rate cutting.

"We remain comfortable with our view that the Fed will not cut rates again under Chair Powell," Bank of America's Adarsh Sinha and Claudio Piron said in a note. They forecast cuts in June, September and December 2026, for a 3%-3.25% terminal rate.

For grains, a lower dollar makes U.S. grains more competitive on the world export market.

AHEAD

  • The USDA is scheduled to release its weekly grain export inspections report at 11 a.m. EST Monday.
  • The EIA is due to release its weekly ethanol production and stocks report at 10:30 a.m. EST Wednesday.
  • The USDA is scheduled to release its weekly export sales report at 8:30 a.m. EST Thursday.
  • Paulo Trevisani contributed to this article.

Write to Kirk Maltais at kirk.maltais@wsj.com

data.title: ATL FED'S BOSTIC Q&A: MORE ENDURING NOT THE CONCRETE ACCOMPLISHMENTS BUT ENCOURAGING A CULTURE THAT SEEKS INSIGHTS; 'CULTURE EATS STRATEGY FOR LUNCH'
Emmaus Life Sciences Reports Quarterly Financial Results
data.description:
TORRANCE, Calif., Nov. 14, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Emmaus Life Sciences, Inc. , a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company and leader in the treatment of sickle cell disease, today reported on its financial condition and results of operations as of and for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2025.

Highlights

"We experienced a 38% decline in net revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2025 as compared to the same period in 2024 due to ongoing competition from generic L-Glutamine in the U.S. and lower sales in the Middle East North Africa, or MENA, region," commented Willis Lee, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Emmaus. "We realized income from operations of $0.7 million compared to $0.8 million in the prior year, partially offset by a 43% reduction in operating expenses," he added.

Financial and Operating Results

Net Revenues. Net revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2025 were $3.4 million, compared to $5.5 million in the same period in 2024. The decrease was due to a decrease in U.S. sales which management attributes to competition from the generic version of L-Glutamine introduced in the market in mid-2024 and a decrease of sales in the MENA region, which management attributes to the timing of sales in the region.

Operating Expenses. Total operating expenses for the three months were $2.4 million compared to $4.3 million in the comparable period in 2024. The decrease was due primarily to decreases in selling expenses and general and administrative expenses attributable to a reduction in force and other cost cutting measures in the second half of 2024.

Income (Loss) From Operations. Income from operations for the three months was $0.7 million compared to $0.8 million in the same period in 2024. This was due to the decrease in net revenues, partially offset by lower operating expenses.

Other Income ( Expense ) . The company realized other expense of $2.2 million for the three months compared to other income of $1.0 million in the same period in 2024. The increase in other expense was due to a decrease of $2.3 million in change in fair value of conversion feature derivative liabilities and increases in $0.6 million in interest expense and $0.3 million in loss on debt extinguishment.

Net Loss. For the three months, the company realized net loss of $2.1 million, or $0.03 per share based on approximately 63.9 million weighted-average basic common shares, compared to net income of $1.8 million, or $0.03 per share based on approximately 63.9 million weighted-average basic common shares in the comparable period in 2024. The increase in net loss was attributable primarily to the increase in other expense.

Liquidity and Capital Resources. At September 30, 2025, the company had cash and cash equivalents of $0.3 million, compared to $1.4 million at December 31, 2024.

About Emmaus Life Sciences

Emmaus Life Sciences, Inc. is a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company and leader in the treatment of sickle cell disease. Endari(R) (L-glutamine oral powder), indicated to reduce the acute complications of sickle cell disease in adults and children 5 years and older, is approved for marketing in the United States, Israel, Kuwait, Qatar, the United Arab Emirates, Bahrain and Oman and is available on a named patient or early access basis in France, the Netherlands, and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, where Emmaus' application for marketing authorization is awaiting final action by the Saudi Food & Drug Authority. For more information, please visit www.emmausmedical.com.

About Endari(R) (prescription grade L-glutamine oral powder)

Endari(R), Emmaus' prescription grade L-glutamine oral powder, was approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) in July 2017 for treating sickle cell disease in adult and pediatric patients five years of age and older.

Indication

Endari(R) is indicated to reduce the acute complications of sickle cell disease in adult and pediatric patients five years of age and older.

Important Safety Information

The most common adverse reactions (incidence >10 percent) in clinical studies were constipation, nausea, headache, abdominal pain, cough, pain in extremities, back pain, and chest pain.

Adverse reactions leading to treatment discontinuation included one case each of hypersplenism, abdominal pain, dyspepsia, burning sensation, and hot flash.

The safety and efficacy of Endari(R) in pediatric patients with sickle cell disease younger than five years of age has not been established.

For more information, please see full Prescribing Information of Endari(R) at: www.ENDARIrx.com/PI.

About Sickle Cell Disease

There are approximately 100,000 people living with sickle cell disease (SCD) in the United States and millions more globally. The sickle gene is found in every ethnic group, not just among those of African descent; and in the United States an estimated 1-in-365 African Americans and 1-in-16,300 Hispanic Americans are born with SCD.(1) The genetic mutation responsible for SCD causes an individual's red blood cells to distort into a "C" or a sickle shape, reducing their ability to transport oxygen throughout the body. These sickled red blood cells break down rapidly, become very sticky, and develop a propensity to clump together, which causes them to become stuck and cause damage within blood vessels. The result is reduced blood flow to distal organs, which leads to physical symptoms of incapacitating pain, tissue and organ damage, and early death.(2)

(1) Source: Data & Statistics on Sickle Cell Disease — National Center on Birth Defects and Developmental Disabilities, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, December 2020.

(2) Source: Committee on Addressing Sickle Cell Disease — A Strategic Plan and Blueprint for Action — National Academy of Sciences Press, 2020.

Forward-looking Statements

This press release contains forward-looking statements made pursuant to the safe harbor provisions of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, as amended. These forward-looking statements are subject to numerous assumptions, risks and uncertainties which change over time, including doubt about the company's ability to continue as a going concern and other factors disclosed in the company's Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024 filed with the SEC on April 14, 2025 and Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q filed on November 14, 2025, and actual results may differ materially. Such forward-looking statements speak only as of the date they are made, and Emmaus assumes no duty to update them, except as may be required by law.

Company Contact:

Emmaus Life Sciences, Inc.

Investor Relations

(310) 214-0065

IR@emmauslifesciences.com

data.title: Williams-Sonoma Is Maintained at Outperform by Telsey Advisory Group
Noble Roman's Announces Significant Third Quarter Earnings Growth and Other Company Highlights
data.description:
INDIANAPOLIS, IN / ACCESS Newswire / November 14, 2025 / Noble Roman's, Inc. (OTCQB:NROM), the Indianapolis based franchisor of Noble Roman's Pizza and Noble Roman's Craft Pizza & Pub, today announced third quarter 2025 financial data, other recent company highlights, and details for a conference call to be held on Monday, November 17th at 4 PM ET.

The company is releasing its third quarter earnings summary now and will be filing its third quarter 10Q once the company's auditors complete their quarterly review, which is currently waiting on a technical determination of the valuation of the company's Warrant Liability. Said Scott Mobley, President of Noble Roman's, "Our new PCAOB auditing firm is conducting their first review for us, and they are understandably requiring extra time to acclimate to the company's business operations. As part of their process, they would like to take a current sounding on the Warrant Liability valuation. This valuation process is complex and requires a third-party specialist firm with expertise in such technical analysis and computation. Since we are very comfortable with the remainder of the process, and since we do not anticipate a material variation in results from the Warrant Liability valuation, and since that valuation is a non-cash, non-operational line item in the financial statements, we wanted to put out the third quarter results now. And as far as those results go, we are extremely pleased to report that both revenue and profitability continue to grow very substantially."

Financial highlights from the third quarter of 2025 include:

  • Net Income Before Taxes grew substantially to $578,918 versus $193,314 for the same period in 2024. Net Income Before Taxes is a key metric since the company will not pay income taxes for a number of years due to its roughly $3.23 million Deferred Tax Asset. Net income was $437,77 in 2025 compared to $193,314 in 2024.

  • Total Revenue of $4.14 million versus $3.88 million for the same period in 2024, an increase of 6.8%.

  • Same stores sales increase in the company-owned Craft Pizza & Pub segment of 4.2% versus the same period in 2024, despite lackluster industry trends and consumer sentiment. The margin contribution rate increased in the second quarter of 2025 to 12.8% from 7.9% for the same period in 2024.

  • The margin contribution from the company's Convenience Store Pizza Program segment was $1,077,042 in 2025 versus $937,916 for the same period in 2024, an increase of 14.8%. The margin contribution rate increased in the second quarter of 2025 to 73.4% versus 65.2% for the same period in 2024. Expenses in this segment actually declined for the three months in 2025 versus 2024; they generally remain relatively stable over significant increments in revenue, so the margin rate increases as overall segment revenue increases.

Financial highlights from the first nine months of 2025 include:

  • Net Income Before Taxes of $1,241,219 versus $163,695 for the same period in 2024. The company will not pay income taxes for a number of years due to its roughly $3.23 million Deferred Tax Asset. Net Income was $939,283 in 2025 versus $163,695 in 2024.

  • Total Revenue of $12.0 million versus $11.5 million for the same period in 2024, an increase of 4.7%.

  • Same stores sales increase in the company-owned Craft Pizza & Pub segment of 3.4% versus the same period in 2024, despite unusually inclement weather during the first quarter of 2025 and lackluster consumer sentiment. The margin contribution rate increased in the first nine months of 2025 to 11.1% from 9.1% for the same period in 2024.

  • The contribution margin from the company's Convenience Store Pizza Program segment was $3,036,009 in 2025 versus $2,848,592 for the same period in 2024, an increase of 6.6%. The contribution margin rate increased in the first nine months of 2025 to 69.5% versus 66.3% for the same period in 2024. Expenses in this segment actually decreased in the first nine months of 2025 versus 2024; they generally remain relatively stable over significant increments in revenue, so the margin rate increases as overall segment revenue increases.

Additional company highlights:

  • Trailing 12-month EBITDA for the year 2024 was approximately $3,060,000; trailing 12-month EBITDA for the period ended March 31, 2025 was approximately $3,135,000; trailing 12-month EBITDA for the period ended June 30, 2025 was approximately $3,501,000; and trailing EBITDA for the period ended September 30, 2025 was approximately $3,825,000.

  • In July, the company introduced its new "Xtra-Stuffed Crust" promotional pizza into its Craft Pizza & Pub segment to slipstream behind the marketing efforts of national competitors promoting a similar product. The company has two additional new pizza promotions readying for deployment at the opportune market time: Spicy Buffalo Chicken Pizza, a premium-priced specialty topping combination, and the 2XL Party Pizza, a jumbo-sized 300 square inch sheet pizza that is 50% larger than its current XL Pizza and scheduled to join the XL Pizza as a value-priced offering.

  • Year-to-date through September 30, 2025, the company has sold nine more franchised Pizza Program units in 2025 than it had opened during that same period, adding to the backlog of sold but unopened units that should contribute significantly to increased franchising revenue. Additionally, the company has a significant pipeline of active prospects for new franchised Pizza Program unit sales. The company anticipates opening up to an additional 27 new franchised Pizza Program locations during the period of September 30th to the end of the year.

  • The company continues to aggressively pursue new financing on terms that would improve on those from its current financing package. Progress appears to be accelerating, and the company is in the process of both evaluating preliminary proposals and pursuing options to the proposal stage. Among other uses, proceeds would be used to retire the current senior loan with Corbel Capital Partners SBIC, L.P., along with their outstanding warrants (which Corbel has indicated their desire to accomplish as well), the company's subordinated notes, and the cost of the capital raise. As previously announced, in April 2025 the company entered into an agreement with Corbel to extend the maturity of their loan to the company until June 30, 2026.

  • As previously announced, the company has engaged Stephano Slack LLC as its new independent auditor for its consolidated financial statements for the year-ended December 31, 2025 as well as the quarterly reviews of its financial statements. Among their wide-ranging services, Stephano Slack provides cost-effective audit services to public companies and middle-market businesses in a range of industries. The auditors for the company's 2023 and 2024 consolidated financial statements expressed an unqualified opinion for both years.

Conference Call details:

The company will host a conference call on Monday, November 17th from 4:00 to 4:30 PM ET. Those interested in participating in the conference call should dial in at 317-300-7896 and use the participation code 499795 (no pin number required). Callers who supplied their first and last names when joining the call will press 5* to ask questions when Q&A time is announced.

The statements concerning the company's future revenues, profitability, financial resources, financing efforts, market demand and product development are forward-looking statements (as such term is defined in the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995) relating to the company that are based on the beliefs of the management of the company, as well as assumptions and estimates made by and information currently available to the company's management. The company's auditing firm has not yet fully completed its review of the third quarter financial statements, and some results, such as the Warrant Liability valuation, may change the results presented in this press release. The company's actual results in the future may differ materially from those indicated by the forward-looking statements due to risks and uncertainties that exist in the company's operations and business environment, including, but not limited to competitive factors and pricing and cost pressures, the company's ability to service its loan and refinance the Senior Note before its maturity in 2026, the emergence or spread of human or animal pandemics (such as COVID-19 or the Avian Influenza), non-renewal of franchise agreements or the openings contemplated by the Development Agreement not occurring, shifts in market demand, the success of franchise programs, general economic conditions and national or international events, changes in demand for the company's products or franchises, the impact of franchise regulation, the success or failure of individual franchisees, inflation, other changes in prices or supplies of food ingredients and labor and as well as the factors discussed under "Risk Factors" contained in the Annual Report on Form 10-K. Should one or more of these risks or uncertainties materialize, or should underlying assumptions or estimates prove incorrect, actual results may vary materially from those described herein as anticipated, believed, estimated, expected or intended. If activist stockholder activities ensue, or if certain parties (acting individually or as a group) seek to continue or initiate interference in the company's business relationships, the company business could be adversely impacted.

END

FOR ADDITIONAL INFORMATION, CONTACT:

For Media Information: Scott Mobley, President & CEO (smobley@nobleromans.com)

For Investor Relations: Paul Mobley, Executive Chairman (pmobley@nobleromans.com)

Mike Cole, Investor Relations: 949-444-1341 (mike.cole@armaadvisoryservices.com)

SOURCE: Noble Romans, Inc.

data.title: Golden Minerals Reports Third Quarter 2025 Financial Results
data.description:
GOLDEN, CO / ACCESS Newswire / November 14, 2025 / Golden Minerals Company ("Golden Minerals", "Golden" or the "Company") (OTCQB:AUMN) has reported financial results and a business summary for the quarter ending September 30, 2025. (All figures are in approximate U.S. dollars.)

Financial Summary for the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025

  • Exploration expenses were $0.3 million as of September 30, 2025, vs. $0.5 million as of September 30, 2024.

  • Administrative expenses were $1.9 million as of September 30, 2025, vs. $3.0 million as of September 30, 2024.

  • Loss from discontinued operations, net of taxes, was $0.5 million as of September 30, 2025, vs. $3.3 million as of September 30, 2024.

  • Net loss was $2.4 million or $0.16 per share as of September 30, 2025, compared to a net loss of $3.8 million or $0.26 per share as of September 30, 2024.

  • Cash and equivalents balance was $1.7 million as of September 30, 2025, vs. $3.2 million as of December 31, 2024.

  • Debt was zero as of September 30, 2025, unchanged from December 31, 2024.

Q3 2025 Business Summary

The Company achieved a significant reduction in liabilities and a meaningful decrease in its cost structure through restructuring efforts initiated in 2024 and continued through the first nine months of 2025. These combined actions strengthened the Company's balance sheet and preserved capital, which will enable the Company to focus on advancing its most prospective exploration assets, as further described below.

The Velardeña Properties transaction closed on October 10, 2025, for a total purchase price of US$3.0 million plus applicable value-added tax (VAT). The full amount has been received, marking the successful completion of the sale. With this closing, the Company has now fully divested its Velardeña operations.

The Desierto Project, located in the Puna geological region of Salta Province, Argentina, has been the subject of surface exploration that has identified alteration zones enriched in clay and silica, characteristics typically associated with precious metal systems. Rock sampling across multiple zones has returned anomalous gold and silver values, and the observed alteration patterns suggest the presence of a potentially larger mineralizing system at depth. The Company anticipates initiating a Phase I drill program targeting extensions of gold mineralization identified at the adjacent Sarita Este Project. The Company is working on joint venture documentation and continues to integrate historical drilling data to refine its regional geological model.

In January 2025, the Company exercised its option to earn-in a 60% interest in the Sand Canyon Project, located in Nevada, pursuant to its agreement with Golden Gryphon Explorations, Inc. The parties are currently finalizing joint venture documentation. While no drilling is planned for 2025, the Company continues to review and integrate historical exploration data and technical studies to support future exploration planning.

2025 Liquidity Discussion

At September 30, 2025, aggregate cash and cash equivalents totaled $1.7 million, compared to $3.2 million at December 31, 2024. The September 30, 2025, decrease is the result of the following expenditures and cash flows for the Nine months ended September 30, 2025.

  • $0.3 million cash spent on discontinued operations, primarily related to care and maintenance cost of the Velardeña properties;

  • $2.9 million in general and administrative and exploration expenditures.

The above expenditures were partially offset by cash inflows of $1.8 million from the following:

  • $1.2 million of proceeds received from the sale of Velardeña Plant 2 and water wells; and

  • $0.6 million from the sale of Minera de Cordilleras.

Capital Resources and 2025 Financial Outlook

The Company does not currently have sufficient resources to meet our expected cash needs for a period of twelve months beyond the filing date of this 2025 Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q. At September 30, 2025, we had current assets of approximately $2.0 million, including cash and cash equivalents of approximately $1.7 million. On the same date, we had accounts payable and other current liabilities of approximately $4.3 million, which includes $2.97 million in deferred revenue for the sale of the Velardeña oxide plant and water wells recorded within Current liabilities held for sale on the interim Condensed Consolidated Balance Sheets. As previously disclosed, the Company ceased mining at the Velardeña mines in Mexico in the first quarter 2024 and subsequently sold the mines and certain related assets. As of September 30, 2025, the Company was owed $28,000 plus $5,000 value-added tax ("VAT") of the $3.0 million purchase price plus VAT for the Velardeña Properties. This remaining amount was received during October 2025 and as a result, the Company will recognize the related gain from sale of Velardeña Properties on its 2025 Annual Report.

The Company's only near-term opportunity to generate cash flow to meet its expected cash requirements is from the sale of assets, equity or other external financing. The Company is evaluating and pursuing alternatives, including the potential sale of the Company, seeking buyers or partners for the Company's other assets or obtaining equity or other external financing. In the absence of additional cash inflows, the Company anticipates that its cash resources will be exhausted in approximately the second quarter of 2026. If we are unable to obtain additional cash resources or sell the Company, we will be forced to cease operations and liquidate.

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release contains forward-looking statements within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and applicable Canadian securities legislation, including statements regarding the Company's forecasted expenditures for 2025; expectations regarding receiving remaining funds owed from the sale of the Velardeña Properties; potential exploration activities and completion of joint venture documentation; the Company's liquidity forecast for 2025; the ability of the Company generate additional cash flow in the near term and the amount of proceeds needed to cover forecasted expenditures; and the Company's expectations regarding the depletion of its cash balance in the Second quarter of 2026 and the potential consequences should depletion occur. These statements are subject to risks and uncertainties, including increases in costs and declines in general economic conditions; delays in exploration activities or completing joint venture documentation; changes in political conditions, in tax, royalty, environmental

and other laws in the United States, Mexico or Argentina and other market conditions; and fluctuations in silver and gold prices. Golden Minerals assumes no obligation to update this information. Additional risks relating to Golden Minerals may be found in the periodic and current reports filed with the SEC by Golden Minerals, including the Company's Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024. For additional information, please visit http://www.goldenminerals.com/ or contact:

Golden Minerals Company

(303) 839-5060

SOURCE: Golden Minerals Company

Hammond Manufacturing Company Limited Appoints Paul Heuts As Chief Financial Officer
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

    GUELPH, ONTARIO – November 14, 2025 - TheNewswire


    To all employees and shareholders:


    We are pleased to announce the appointment of Paul
    Heuts as Chief Financial Officer, effective November 17, 2025. Paul
    succeeds Alex Stirling, who has served as interim CFO and will return
    to his role as Executive Vice President.


    Paul brings over 18 years of experience in finance and
    manufacturing operations, most recently serving as Vice President
    Finance, Connection Technologies (AmerCable, Shawflex, DSG-Canusa) at
    Mattr. His expertise includes financial reporting, audits, internal
    controls, strategic planning, treasury, taxation, and risk
    management.


    In his latest role, Paul was accountable for six
    P&Ls across four countries (Canada, U.S., Germany, and China),
    demonstrating his ability to lead complex, global financial
    operations.


    “Paul’s proven track record in financial leadership
    and operational excellence makes him an outstanding addition to our
    executive team,” said Rob Hammond, Chief Executive Officer of
    Hammond Manufacturing. “We look forward to his contributions as we
    continue to strengthen our financial strategy and deliver long-term
    value to our shareholders.”


    Hammond Manufacturing Company Limited manufactures a
    broad range of products for the electronic and electrical products
    industry, including metallic and non-metallic enclosures, racks, small
    cases, outlet strips, surge suppressors and electronic
    transformers.


    For information, contact:


    Hammond Manufacturing Company Limited


    Robert F. Hammond, Chairman and CEO


    Tel. (519) 822-2960


    Fax. (519) 822-7289


    Email:  ir@hammfg.com

    Copyright (c) 2025 TheNewswire - All rights reserved.

data.title: Dir Shoemaker Files To Sell 1,000 Of ETHZilla Corp [ETHZ]
data.description:
Shoemaker Stephen H, Director of ETHZilla Corp [ETHZ], filed a Form 144 on November 14, 2025 with the Securities and Exchange Commission proposing to sell restricted securities:

Approximate Date Of Sale11/14/25
Shares1,000
BrokerOppenheimer & Co Inc

Prior to the sale of restricted stock, owners must file a Form 144 with the SEC. Upon submission of the filing to the SEC the owner is permitted to sell the shares, or any fraction thereof, within 90 days.

data.title: ATL FED'S BOSTIC Q&A: NEED DIVERSITY IN LOOKING FOR TALENT, SOLUTIONS, IN RURAL AS WELL AS URBAN AREAS; 'CAN FIND DIAMONDS IN THE ROUGH'
CBOT corn falls after release of USDA data
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade corn futures dipped on Friday after the release of long-awaited supply-demand numbers from the U.S. Department of Agriculture, which were suspended during the 43-day government shutdown.

  • CBOT December corn (CZ25) settled down 11-1/4 cents at $4.30-1/4 per bushel.

  • The USDA pegged the average 2025 U.S. corn yield at 186.0 bushels per acre, down from 186.7 on September 12. It lowered its production estimate to 16.752 billion bushels, from 16.814 billion in September.

  • Traders had expected the government to forecast deeper cuts to the nation's corn crop, according to analysts.

  • Ahead of Friday's USDA supply/demand reports, the first since mid-September, analysts surveyed by Reuters on average expected the government to lower its estimate of the U.S. corn yield to 184.0 bushels per acre.

  • The range of estimates was unusually wide, reflecting the impact of a USDA data blackout during the 43-day U.S. government shutdown that ended this week.

data.title: Pacific Oak Strategic Opportunity REIT, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Pacific Oak Strategic Opportunity REIT, Inc., a real estate investment trust specializing in strategic opportunistic properties, residential homes, and hotels, has released its Form 10-Q for the third quarter of 2025. The report reveals significant financial and operational challenges, including a substantial net loss and increased impairment charges, amid a difficult market environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $28.5 million, a decrease from $32.4 million in the prior year period, primarily due to the disposition of properties.
  • Net Loss: $119.9 million, an increase from a net loss of $45.7 million in the prior year period, driven by higher impairment charges on real estate and related intangibles.
  • Impairment Charges on Real Estate and Related Intangibles: $76.8 million, significantly up from $15.8 million in the prior year period, due to declines in market conditions and projected cash flows.
  • Interest Expense, Net: $19.8 million, a slight decrease from $20.6 million in the prior year period, primarily due to property dispositions.
  • Foreign Currency Transaction Loss, Net: $5.9 million, an increase from $4.6 million in the prior year period, primarily due to unfavorable exchange rates affecting the Series Bonds denominated in Israeli new shekels.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: For the three months ended September 30, 2025, the strategic opportunistic properties segment generated $17.8 million in revenue, residential homes generated $9.7 million, and the hotel segment generated $1.0 million.
  • Geographical Performance: As of September 30, 2025, the company's real estate investments in Texas, Tennessee, and California represented 13%, 12%, and 11% of the company's total assets, respectively. This geographic concentration makes the company susceptible to adverse economic developments in these markets.
  • Sales Units: During the nine months ended September 30, 2025, the company sold 17 residential homes and one strategic opportunistic property. Additionally, two strategic opportunistic properties met the held-for-sale criteria.
  • Future Outlook: The company is facing substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern due to upcoming loan maturities and challenging market conditions. Management plans to utilize extension options, refinance debt, market properties for sale, or negotiate property turnovers to address these challenges.
  • Operational Challenges: The company is experiencing difficulties due to elevated interest rates, which have increased financing costs and made refinancing debt obligations challenging. The company is also dealing with occupancy challenges, with office complexes being 66% occupied as of September 30, 2025.
  • Impairment Charges: The company recorded significant impairment charges on real estate and related intangibles, totaling $76.8 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, due to declines in market conditions and projected cash flows.
  • Strategic Initiatives: On October 14, 2025, the board of directors formed a special committee to explore strategic alternatives in light of the company's financial situation, including ongoing negotiations with bondholders.
  • Lease Obligations: The company has a leasehold interest expiring in 2114, with future minimum lease payments totaling $52.7 million as of September 30, 2025.
  • Contractual Commitments: The company has outstanding debt obligations of $877.0 million, with significant maturities scheduled over the next year, creating liquidity challenges.

SEC Filing: Pacific Oak Strategic Opportunity REIT, Inc. [ PCOK ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: DSS, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
DSS, INC., a diversified holding company with interests in various sectors including product packaging, biotechnology, commercial lending, securities, and direct marketing, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in financial performance and provides insights into the company's operational achievements and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $6.39 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, reflecting an increase from $5.60 million in the same period of 2024.

Total Revenue: $16.63 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $13.68 million in the same period of 2024.

Operating Loss: $(3.23) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, an improvement from $(4.67) million in the same period of 2024.

Operating Loss: $(10.38) million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(14.29) million in the same period of 2024.

Net Loss: $(2.23) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(5.70) million in the same period of 2024.

Net Loss: $(10.09) million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(15.76) million in the same period of 2024.

Net Loss Attributable to DSS Common Stockholders: $(2.30) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(5.28) million in the same period of 2024.

Net Loss Attributable to DSS Common Stockholders: $(9.18) million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(14.03) million in the same period of 2024.

Loss Per Common Share Attributable to Common Stockholders - Basic and Diluted: $(0.25) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.75) in the same period of 2024.

Loss Per Common Share Attributable to Common Stockholders - Basic and Diluted: $(1.03) for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(1.99) in the same period of 2024.

Business Highlights

Product Packaging Segment: The Product Packaging line, led by Premier Packaging Corporation, operates in the paperboard and fiber-based folding carton markets, providing sophisticated custom folding cartons and document security printing solutions. This segment primarily serves the US market from its facility in Rochester, NY.

Biotechnology Segment: The Biotechnology business line focuses on investing in or acquiring companies in the BioHealth and BioMedical fields, targeting drug discovery and treatment of neurological, oncological, and immune-related diseases. It is also developing open-air defense initiatives to curb airborne infectious diseases.

Commercial Lending Segment: Driven by American Pacific Financial, this segment is organized to acquire equity positions in undervalued commercial banks and nonbanking financial companies across the US and Asia. It offers services like loan syndication, mortgage banking, and advisory capital raising.

Securities and Investment Management Segment: This segment develops and acquires assets in securities trading and management, including broker-dealers and mutual funds. It also includes a real estate investment trust (REIT) focused on acquiring and leasing medical real estate.

Direct Marketing Segment: Led by Decentralized Sharing Systems, this segment provides services for peer-to-peer decentralized sharing marketplaces, offering nutritional and personal care products across North America, Asia Pacific, and Eastern Europe.

Geographical Performance: The company operates globally with significant activities in the US, South East Asia, Taiwan, Japan, and South Korea, reflecting its diverse market presence.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue evaluating its merger and share exchange agreement with Dr. Ashleys Limited, pending regulatory approval. Management is focused on reducing expenses and cash burn across all business lines to ensure sustainability.

SEC Filing: DSS, INC. [ DSS ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: 21Shares Ethereum ETF SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
21Shares Ethereum ETF, a Delaware statutory trust operating as an exchange-traded fund (ETF) that tracks the performance of ether, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and operational developments, reflecting the company's strategic initiatives and market performance.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Asset Value (NAV): The NAV increased to $45.91 million, up from $23.00 million on June 30, 2025. This growth was primarily driven by a 65.37% increase in the price of ether and a net increase in shares outstanding.
  • Net Increase in Net Assets from Operations: For the three months ended September 30, 2025, the net increase was $16.96 million, attributed to changes in unrealized appreciation on investment in ether and net realized gains on ether sold for redemptions.
  • Net Increase in Net Assets from Operations: For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, the net increase was $13.55 million, resulting from changes in unrealized appreciation on investment in ether and net realized gains on ether sold for redemptions.
  • Sponsor Fee: The Sponsor fee was $22,761 for the three months ended September 30, 2025, and $38,251 for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, reflecting the cost of services performed under the Trust Agreement.

Business Highlights

  • Trust Overview: The 21Shares Ethereum ETF, formed on September 5, 2023, operates as an ETF issuing units of beneficial interest representing fractional undivided beneficial interests in its net assets. The shares trade on the Cboe BZX Exchange under the ticker symbol 'TETH'.
  • Investment Objective: The Trust aims to track the performance of ether, as measured by the CME CF Ether-Dollar Reference Rate — New York Variant, adjusted for the Trust’s expenses and other liabilities. It also seeks to reflect rewards from staking a portion of the Trust’s ether, subject to legal and regulatory considerations.
  • Operational Structure: Managed and controlled by 21Shares US LLC, a wholly owned subsidiary of Jura Pentium Inc., the Trust's ether is held by custodians including Coinbase Custody Trust Company, LLC, BitGo New York Trust Company, LLC, and Anchorage Digital Bank N.A.
  • Sponsor Fee and Expenses: The Trust pays a unitary Sponsor fee of 0.21% of its ether holdings. The Sponsor waived this fee for the first six months after the Trust's shares were listed or until the Trust's assets reached $500 million, whichever came first. The waiver period ended on January 23, 2025.
  • Creation and Redemption of Shares: Shares are created and redeemed in Creation Baskets of 10,000 shares or multiples thereof. Only Authorized Participants, who are registered broker-dealers, can place orders for creation and redemption.
  • Staking Services Agreement: On October 7, 2025, the Trust entered into a Master Infrastructure-As-A-Service Agreement with Coinbase Crypto Services, LLC to allow for staking of the Trust’s ether. The Trust will receive a portion of the gross staking consideration.
  • Future Outlook: The Trust plans to continue tracking the performance of ether and may engage in staking activities to enhance returns, provided it does not pose undue legal or regulatory risks.

SEC Filing: 21Shares Ethereum ETF [ TETH ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: ATL FED'S BOSTIC Q&A: IMPORTANT TO HEAR FROM THOSE ACTUALLY FACING THE CHALLENGES TO DETERMINE POLICY; LOOK BEYOND WHERE THE LIGHT IS BRIGHTEST
'Buy the Dip' Is Still Alive. The AI Trade Is Too. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Avi Salzman

Ominous clouds gathered on Wall Street this week — and may have passed just as quickly. It's a sign that investors aren't ready to abandon this remarkably persistent bull market.

Despite a sharp selloff on Thursday, and lots of intraday volatility, the S&P 500 index was poised to end the week up 0.2% and just 2% below its all-time high. The Nasdaq Composite, which had fallen three days in a row, also bounced back on Friday, and was set to finish the week off just 0.4%. The Dow Jones Industrial Average was the strongest of the bunch: The blue chip benchmark was up 0.5% for the week and less than a percentage point away from the all-time high it hit on Wednesday.

"There is still a buy-the-dip mind-set," said Luca Paolini, chief strategist at Pictet Asset Management. "A lot of investors have missed the rally." There is a lot of money waiting to pounce on any selloff.

Which isn't to say everything has been perfect. The market has been lifted by two themes of late — artificial-intelligence growth and interest rate cuts from the Federal Reserve — and both faltered this past week. Fed board members questioned whether it makes sense to cut rates again in December, and traders now see only a roughly 50% chance of that happening.

And there were some signs of trouble in the AI world. Data-center owner Coreweave said on Monday that it's having trouble getting its data centers ready fast enough because of a supply-chain problem at a third-party developer. The stock dropped 16% the next day.

If AI development slows while interest rates remain elevated, the market's two problems could compound. Some tech companies building data centers have taken out debt to fund their aspirations, so they would get hit extra-hard by high interest rates on that debt. Debt-heavy companies have been particularly vulnerable to the selloff over the past week. Among the most prominent decliners were Coreweave and software giant Oracle. The fear is that the AI bubble will pop, the debt will come due, and AI tools won't be pumping out enough cash to cover it all.

But there's no sign yet that the bubble is popping, and Big Tech firms generate enough cash from their core operations to cover their capital needs for years, bulls note. Despite all the AI spending, analyst estimates for tech earnings over the next year have risen 9% over the past three months, twice as much as the broader S&P 500, notes Keith Lerner, chief investment officer at Truist Advisory Services.

"Every bull market has a dominant theme," Lerner said. "This one is AI and tech. In a rising market, leadership tends to endure. If you believe the bull market is intact, it's too early to give up on tech."

That's not to say that the direction will be straight up. The market's performance has been remarkable since April, with the S&P 500 rising 38% and semiconductors up over 120%. In that period, there haven't been any pullbacks that took the S&P 500 down 5% or more. Historically, those kinds of pullbacks happen every 77 days, says Lerner.

Next week Nvidia will report its third-quarter earnings. The results will likely determine whether the dark clouds are gone for good, or whether they could hang around a while longer.

Write to Avi Salzman at avi.salzman@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Brazil's Prumo sells stake in joint venture with Anglo American
data.description:
Brazilian logistics firm Prumo said on Friday it agreed to sell its 50% stake in Ferroport, a joint venture with miner Anglo American that operates an iron ore terminal at Rio de Janeiro's Acu Port.

  • The Ferroport stake is being sold to investment firm 3Point2, which had financial support from lender BTG Pactual , Prumo said in a statement.

  • Financial details were not disclosed.

  • Closing of the deal still depends on some conditions, Prumo added.

  • "The transaction is in line with Prumo's strategy of simplifying its corporate structure and optimizing its capital structure," it said.

data.title: UN says Israeli wall crosses Lebanon border
data.description:
By Jasper Ward

A survey conducted by the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon last month found that a wall built by the Israeli military crosses the Blue Line, the de facto border, a U.N. spokesperson said on Friday

The Blue Line is a U.N.-mapped line separating Lebanon from Israel and the Israeli-occupied Golan Heights.

Stephane Dujarric, the spokesperson for the U.N. secretary-general, said the concrete T-wall erected by the IDF has made more than 4,000 square meters (nearly an acre) of Lebanese territory inaccessible to the local population.

A section of an additional wall, which has also crossed the Blue Line, is being erected southeast of Yaroun, he said, citing the peacekeepers.

Dujarric said UNIFIL informed the Israeli military of its findings and requested that the wall be removed.

"Israeli presence and construction in Lebanese territory are violations of Security Council resolution 1701 and of Lebanon’s sovereignty and territorial integrity," UNIFIL said in a separate statement.

An Israeli military spokesperson denied the wall crossed the Blue Line.

"The wall is part of a broader IDF plan whose construction began in 2022. Since the start of the war, and as part of lessons learned from it, the IDF has been advancing a series of measures, including reinforcing the physical barrier along the northern border," the spokesperson said.

UNIFIL, established in 1978, operates between the Litani River in the north and the Blue Line in the south. The mission has more than 10,000 troops from 50 countries and about 800 civilian staff, according to its website.

Treasury Yields Rise as Delayed Data Releases Get Scheduled — Market Talk
data.description: Treasury yields rise as the stream of data withheld during the government shutdown draws near. The much-expected September jobs report is scheduled for Thursday, following releases on construction-spending, manufacturers' orders and international-trade flows earlier in the week. Fed Governor Miran reinstates his argument for interest rate cuts in a Fox Business interview, suggesting his colleagues' worries about inflation are misplaced. As of now, future markets indicate investors are mostly pricing in a hold next month. The 10-year yield rises 0.055 percentage point this week, to 4.147% and the two-year adds 0.057 p.p. to 3.613%. Both are also up for the day, reversing early declines. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.co; @ptrevisani)

data.title: Hogs Settle Positive Following WASDE Release — Market Talk
data.description: Lean hog futures settled up 0.2% to 78.5 cents a pound, which pared the gains clocked earlier in the trading session but kept the hog contract above water. The higher close makes it the first session out of the past four that hog futures have settled higher. Big for hog futures was the release of the USDA's WASDE report, which showed lower hog slaughter through the rest of the year, offsetting the effect of higher weights for hog carcasses. But in 2026, beef production is also expected to slide. High prices and consumer demand for meat has customers buying less beef for holiday use. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)
